stemHtml: Surface visibility at a destination in the centre of France in August is reported as 1 500 metres in mist, with an inversion at 500 feet above aerodrome level and sky clear above. The pilot makes a non-precision approach to runway 18 shortly before sunset with a minimum descent altitude of 600 feet above aerodrome level. At what range would the pilot expect to see the runway and why?

options.a: More than 1 500 m because the aircraft will still be flying above the mist.
options.b: Less than 1 500 m because the Sun in the pilot's eyes will reduce the ability to distinguish objects and therefore air-to-ground visibility.
options.c: Less than 1 500 m because the sunlight reflecting from the mist particles will reduce flight visibility.
options.d: Less than 1 500 m because the sunlight reflecting from the top of the mist layer will reduce air-to-ground visibility.
